content.problemStatement:
Read problem statements in Vietnamese,Given NNN points in a 2D2D2D space, find the minimum sum of areas of rectangles required to cover all the points given that we can use at most 222 non-overlapping rectangles whose sides can touch. The rectangles must be axis-aligned, meaning the sides are vertical and horizontal.

###Input


The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains N+1N + 1N+1 lines of input.
The first line contains a single integer NNN, the number of points.
The next NNN lines each contains two integers xix_ixi‚Äã, yiy_iyi‚Äã, the coordinates of the iii-th point.
**Note:** Points need not be distinct.

###Output
For each test case, output in a single line the answer to the problem.

###Constraints


1‚â§T‚â§1051 \leq T \leq 10^51‚â§T‚â§105
1‚â§N‚â§1051 \leq N \leq 10^51‚â§N‚â§105
0‚â§xi,yi‚â§1090 \leq x_i, y_i \leq 10^90‚â§xi‚Äã,yi‚Äã‚â§109
The sum of NNN over all test cases is at most 10510^5105.
Subtasks

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Case 1: Since there is only one point, the minimum area of a rectangle to cover this point is 000.
Case 2: We can have rectangles as 222 opposite sides of the square. Since the width of the rectangles is 000, the total area is also 000.

Case 3: The optimal solution is with the rectangles having endpoints {(1,1),(100,1),(1,50),(100,50)}\{(1, 1), (100, 1), (1, 50), (100, 50)\}{(1,1),(100,1),(1,50),(100,50)} and {(1,100),(1,100),(100,100),(100,100)}\{(1, 100), (1, 100), (100, 100), (100, 100)\}{(1,100),(1,100),(100,100),(100,100)}. Therefore the total area is 49‚ãÖ99+0‚ãÖ99=485149 \cdot 99 + 0 \cdot 99 = 485149‚ãÖ99+0‚ãÖ99=4851
